title: Fix 401 Unauthorized error on admin CRUD operations

requirements.summary: Ensure actor is initialized with authenticated identity before mutations
acceptanceCriteria: After login, the actor used by mutation hooks carries the authenticated Internet Identity principal, not the anonymous identity.
If a user logs in and immediately attempts to create a resource, the actor is initialized with the correct identity before the call is made.
Logging out and back in produces a fresh actor with the new identity.
No mutation hook in useQueries.ts falls back silently to an anonymous actor when an authenticated identity is available.

file_operations.description: Add explicit identity validation to ensure the actor wrapper never returns an actor initialized with an anonymous identity when an authenticated identity is available from the InternetIdentityProvider. Add a state check that waits for identity resolution before returning the actor, preventing race conditions where mutations are dispatched before authentication completes.

Add explicit identity checks to all mutation hooks (addSkill, updateSkill, deleteSkill, createProject, updateProject, deleteProject, createService, updateService, deleteService, addBlog, updateBlog, deleteBlog, createCategory, updateCategory, deleteCategory, createSocialLink, updateSocialLink, deleteSocialLink, setSeoSetting, deleteSeoSetting, addTestimonial, updateTestimonial, deleteTestimonial) to ensure the actor is available and authenticated before dispatching the mutation. Include error handling that prevents mutations from executing with an anonymous identity.

requirements.summary: Fix AdminGuard admin status verification and state persistence
acceptanceCriteria: AdminGuard correctly confirms admin status on each protected route before rendering the management UI.
The admin status is not lost on page navigation or React Query cache invalidation.
If the user is not admin, AdminGuard shows the appropriate UI instead of silently allowing mutations that will fail.

file_operations.description: Replace the imperative useEffect-based admin status check with a proper React Query hook (useIsCallerAdmin) to ensure admin status is cached and persisted across navigations. Add dependency on the authenticated identity to trigger re-verification when the user logs in or out. Ensure the guard waits for both actor initialization and admin status resolution before rendering admin-protected content or showing the access denied UI.

Ensure the useIsCallerAdmin query hook (if not already present, add it) uses proper query key scoping and is enabled only when the actor is available. Configure the query to use staleTime and cacheTime so admin status persists across page navigations without unnecessary re-fetches, while still invalidating when the identity changes.
